paragraphs.text: The most common type of braces, modern metal brackets are smaller and more comfortable than before. They provide reliable, efficient tooth movement for children, teens, and adults who want a predictable path to a straight smile.

image.alt: Close-up of traditional metal braces on teeth

paragraphs.text: Ceramic or tooth-colored braces blend with your smile for a less noticeable look while still delivering effective tooth movement. They are a popular choice for patients seeking a more discreet fixed appliance.
Clear aligners use a series of removable, custom trays to straighten teeth gradually. They let you eat and clean normally and are an excellent option for patients who want a low‑profile treatment.

image.alt: Two stylish smiling teens taking a selfie

paragraphs.text: Invisalign<sup>®</sup> uses 3‑D imaging to create a sequence of custom clear aligners. Each tray is worn for about two weeks, allowing discreet, controlled movement with minimal impact on daily life.

image.alt: Close-up of clear aligners being placed on teeth

paragraphs.text: Iconix Champagne Gold Braces combine the strength of metal brackets with a subtle, champagne‑gold finish for a more cosmetic appearance while maintaining dependable tooth movement.
The Carriere Self‑Ligating Bracket System is designed for comfort and efficiency. By reducing friction and the need for elastic ties, it can shorten treatment time for many patients.

image.alt: Orthodontic office interior showing treatment equipment

paragraphs.text: A palatal expander gently widens the upper jaw by applying pressure to the molars at adjustment. It creates space for alignment and can help correct crossbites and crowded bites when used at the right stage of development.

Surgical orthodontics, or orthognathic surgery, is used to correct severe jaw and bite discrepancies. We coordinate closely with oral and maxillofacial surgeons to plan combined surgical and orthodontic care when it will improve function and facial balance.

paragraphs.text: Orthodontics (also referred to as dentofacial orthopedics) is a specialized form of dentistry that focuses on the diagnosis, prevention, and treatment of dental and facial abnormalities.

An orthodontist is a dental specialist who has received two to three years of additional training and experience. Dr. Rocha is able to straighten teeth, correct misaligned jaw structure, and improve the function of your smile.

accordion.header: WHAT’S THE BEST AGE TO VISIT THE ORTHODONTIST?

paragraphs.text: If you want to improve the look and feel of your smile, then any age can be a great time to see the orthodontist. The American Association of Orthodontists recommends that children first visit an orthodontist around the age of seven; however, orthodontic treatment is not exclusive to children and teens. About one in every five orthodontic patients is over the age of 21. (Dr. Rocha has even improved the smiles of patients in their 80s!) Whether you’re considering treatment for yourself or for a child, any age is a good time to visit the orthodontist.

accordion.header: HOW CAN I TAKE CARE OF MY TEETH IF I’M WEARING BRACES OR A RETAINER?

paragraphs.text:
• ALWAYS remember to brush your teeth after every meal and floss at least once a day.
  • Make sure to use toothpaste that contains fluoride, and ask your family dentist if you need a fluoride rinse. This will help prevent cavities!
  • If you take out your retainer to eat, make sure you brush your teeth, floss, and remember to keep your retainer safe in its container so that it does not get lost or broken.
  • Keep your retainer clean, too, by brushing it gently with a clean, wet toothbrush. You may also soak it in retainer cleaner or half white vinegar/half water as instructed by Dr. Rocha. Do not put your retainer in boiling water or in the dishwasher.
  • During your treatment, try to avoid foods with a lot of sugar, which increases the amount of bacteria that grows in your mouth, and can cause more plaque and potential cavities.
  • Avoid sticky and chewy foods (caramel, chewing gum, gummy bears), hard foods (hard candy, nuts, ice cubes), or any foods that could possibly get stuck in your braces (corn on the cob, bagels, ribs, taffy, etc.).
  • Be sure to schedule your routine checkups with your family dentist. It is recommended that you continue to visit the dentist every six months.
Braces are used by an orthodontist to help you improve the look and feel of your smile. There are several different types of braces to choose from, including:

• Clear braces
  • Ceramic braces
  • Self-ligating braces
  • Invisible braces
  • Traditional metal braces

accordion.header: IF I GET BRACES OR INVISALIGN, HOW LONG DO I HAVE TO WEAR THEM?

paragraphs.text: The amount of time spent in braces will vary, depending on the individual patient, because every smile responds differently to treatment. Treatment times can take anywhere from six to 30 months, but most standard treatments last about 18 months.
Braces do not often hurt, though you may feel a small amount of discomfort for a couple days as your teeth, gums, cheeks, and mouth get used to your new braces.

accordion.header: DO I NEED TO BRUSH MY TEETH MORE OFTEN IF I HAVE BRACES/INVISALIGN?

paragraphs.text: With braces, you should brush your teeth at least three times a day to keep your teeth, gums, and mouth healthy and clean. Brushing regularly will help remove any food that may be caught between your braces.
You should also floss daily to get between your braces where your brush isn’t able to reach. Dr. Rocha can show you how to brush and floss properly once your braces are placed.

accordion.header: IF I HAVE BRACES/INVISALIGN, DO I STILL NEED DENTAL CHECKUPS EVERY SIX MONTHS?

paragraphs.text: Yes! In fact, it’s even more essential that patients receiving orthodontic treatment visit their dentist regularly. With braces, food may be caught in places your toothbrush can’t reach. This causes bacteria to build up and can lead to cavities, gingivitis, and gum disease. Dr. Rocha will work closely with your dentist to make sure your teeth stay clean and healthy while you’re wearing braces.

accordion.header: WILL MY BRACES INTERFERE WITH MY SCHOOL ACTIVITIES LIKE SPORTS, PLAYING AN INSTRUMENT, OR SINGING?

paragraphs.text: Playing an instrument or a contact sport may require some adjustment when you first get your braces, but wearing braces will not stop you from participating in any of your school activities. If you play a contact sport, it is recommended that you wear a mouthguard to protect your braces or appliance.

accordion.header: HOW DO I SCHEDULE MY NEXT APPOINTMENT?

paragraphs.text: Simply call/text/email our practice! Our front desk staff will be happy to schedule your next appointment at your convenience. If you are a new patient or have been referred to our practice, please let us know and we will provide you with all the information you need.

paragraphs.text: Iconix braces are an advanced type of metal braces designed to provide dependable tooth movement while offering a more cosmetic look. They deliver the same proven orthodontic performance as traditional braces, but with a unique champagne-gold tone that makes them visually appealing. Many patients choose Iconix braces because they combine durability, comfort, and a more stylish appearance in one treatment option.

accordion.header: How do Iconix Braces Compare to Traditional Braces?

paragraphs.text: Iconix braces function in a very similar way to traditional braces, using brackets and wires to guide teeth into better alignment over time. The key difference is in how they look. Traditional braces typically have a bright silver appearance that can stand out, while Iconix braces offer a softer, champagne-gold color that blends more naturally. This makes them a great option for patients who want the proven effectiveness of metal braces with a more polished and modern appearance.

accordion.header: Why are Iconix Braces Known as "Champagne Braces"?

paragraphs.text: Iconix braces are often referred to as “Champagne Braces” because of their signature champagne-colored finish. This soft gold tone gives the brackets a more elegant look than typical metal braces and helps them appear less bold on the teeth. The color is designed to create a subtle, sophisticated appearance, making Iconix braces an appealing choice for patients who want braces that feel more discreet and aesthetic.

accordion.header: Are Iconix Braces Pricier than Other Types of Braces?

paragraphs.text: The price of Iconix braces can vary depending on the complexity of your orthodontic needs and the overall length of your treatment. In many cases, Iconix braces may be slightly more expensive than standard metal braces because they are considered a cosmetic upgrade. However, they are often viewed as a worthwhile investment for patients who want a more aesthetic option while still receiving reliable orthodontic care. The best way to determine exact pricing is to schedule a consultation and receive a personalized treatment recommendation.

Iconix braces provide several advantages for patients who want a balance between appearance and performance. The champagne-gold finish makes them less visually noticeable than standard metal braces, while still offering the strength needed for effective orthodontic correction. These braces are built to support consistent tooth movement throughout treatment, allowing you to achieve excellent results without sacrificing the look of your smile while you wear them.

accordion.header: Do Iconix Braces Require Special Care?

paragraphs.text: Iconix braces do not require special maintenance beyond good daily oral hygiene and routine orthodontic visits. Brushing thoroughly, flossing consistently, and following your orthodontist’s advice will help keep your teeth healthy and your braces clean throughout treatment. With proper care, your braces can stay in good condition and your orthodontic progress can remain steady, helping you reach your final results as smoothly as possible.

accordion.header: Your Smile Transformation: Iconix Braces Await!

paragraphs.text: Iconix® Champagne Gold Braces offer an excellent solution for patients who want an orthodontic treatment that is both effective and visually appealing. Their champagne-gold finish provides a unique and elegant alternative to traditional metal braces, while still delivering the results you expect from a trusted braces system. If you are considering Iconix braces, scheduling a consultation is a great next step to explore your options and begin your journey toward a confident, beautiful smile.

paragraphs.text: Clear aligners are one of the most modern and popular treatment options for straightening teeth. Using a custom-made series of aligners, your teeth are gradually and gently shifted into their desired location. These trays eliminate the need for metal brackets and wires that traditional braces offer. With clear aligners, all you have to do is wear your trays for 20-22 hours a day and change them once every two weeks until your treatment is finished. This allows you to achieve your dream smile with little disruption to your daily life. Because these trays are removable, you can continue to eat your favorite foods and brush and floss normally. The best part? Clear aligners are virtually invisible so most people don’t even realize you’re undergoing treatment!

image.alt: doctor with patient in orthodontic setting
doctor with patient in orthodontic setting

tabs.header: Is Dr. Rocha Experienced With Clear Aligners?

paragraphs.text: Our office is proud to announce that Dr. Rocha has extensive experience treating patients of all ages, starting as young as 7 years old! Dr. Rocha attended a rigorous post graduate residency program that has one of the strongest clear aligner programs, being taught under orthodontists who helped create clear aligners! Dr. Rocha introduced the Itero 3-D digital model scanner to Valley orthodontics, which will allow in office scans of patients teeth. No more messy, uncomfortable dental impressions or visits to the lab for digital scans!

image.alt: doctor with patient in orthodontic setting

paragraphs.text:
Clear aligners utilize 3-D computer imaging technology to transform impressions of your teeth into a series of customized plastic aligners.

Each aligner is worn for approximately two weeks. They are removed only to eat, brush, and floss.

As you replace each aligner with the next, your teeth will move – little by little, week by week – until they reach the final alignment that Dr. Rocha has prescribed.

The number of aligners you wear depends on the severity of crowding or spacing you have.

image.alt: doctor with patient in orthodontic setting

tabs.header: Are Clear Aligners Right For Me?

paragraphs.text: Clear aligners have been proven effective for adults, teenagers, and even children in clinical research and practices nationwide. Dr. Rocha can determine if this treatment is an effective option for you.

image.alt: doctor with patient in orthodontic setting

tabs.header: How Much Do Clear Aligners Cost?

paragraphs.text: At Valley Orthodontics, clear aligners cost the same as traditional braces. We want to help our patients choose the treatment that is right for them.

image.alt: doctor with patient in orthodontic setting

tabs.header: What Do I Need To Get Started?

paragraphs.text:
At your initial appointment we will discuss your concerns and determine a treatment plan that is right for you. Next, an appointment for your 3D scan can be scheduled as soon as possible. Dr. Rocha determines how your finished result will be accomplished. Once the treatment plan is finalized, the aligners are fabricated and shipped and we can get started.

Consider clear aligners to get the beautiful straight teeth you’ve always wanted – without braces. A consultation with Dr. Rocha can determine if clear aligners are right for you. Whether you’re from San Rafael, Marin, Mill Valley, Novato, or a nearby area, contact us for your clear aligner consultation.

items.text: Patients love this nifty floss for braces. The thin arm on each flossing pick easily slips behind the arch wire, enabling you to floss braces in under 2 minutes! This means no more threading between each tooth! UCLA Study demonstrated that GumChucks makes flossing so easy and fast, that 73.7% of people who tried GumChucks became regular daily flossers!

items.title: Ultrasonic Retainer Cleaner, Retainer Cleaner Machine for Dentures, Mouth Guards,
items.text: Invisalign and retainer patients love this deep cleaner! The Ultrasonic Retainer Cleaner employs high-frequency 45kHz sound waves, effortlessly removing dirt from retainers, mouth guards, and dental appliances, reaching all the hard-to-clean corners and crevices without causing any harm to them. This is a great addition to the daily brushing of retainers/appliances.

image.alt: Ultrasonic Retainer Cleaner, Retainer Cleaner Machine for Dentures, Mouth Guards,

items.title: Retainer Cleaner Tablets, Invisalign Cleaner, Remove Stains, Odors & Plaque
items.text: These retainer cleaning tablets are a great addition to the daily brushing to get out the stubborn stains and build up. Weiss Naturals Retainer Cleaner has fast cleaning results—Just 1 Tablet a Day! Say goodbye to stains, watch grime vanish, and witness your appliance restoring its shine effortlessly. Bonus- they also help with eliminating bad odor: Achieve fresh and odor-free results within just 15 minutes of soaking, guaranteed every time.

image.alt: Retainer Cleaner Tablets, Invisalign Cleaner, Remove Stains, Odors & Plaque

items.title: Clear aligner removal tool
items.text: Helpful tool for removing Invisalign or clear retainers. Especially helpful for those with short and/or thin nails. Bonus chewie surface on opposing end!

items.title: Interdental Brush Toothpick "Proxy brush"
items.text: Great tool to use with braces or cemented appliances to poke out foot that is underneath the wires. They are a great cleaning tool to keep in the backpack/purse/car!

items.title: GUM Orthodontic Wax for Braces and Dental Device
items.text: Braces supply must-have! GUM Ortho Wax provides a comfortable barrier that prevents mouth pain and irritation caused by braces, partial dentures, and orthodontic appliances. This is a helpful tool to keep handy for all braces and cemented appliance patients, especially during the initial weeks of adjusting to your new appliance. Bonus- this wax has a mint flavor that soothes discomfort with a healing blend of vitamin E and Aloe Vera

image.alt: GUM Orthodontic Wax for Braces and Dental Device

items.title: OrthoDots CLEAR – Moisture Activated Braces
items.text: Wax OrthoDots are a favorite by our patients, a newer line of dental wax that sticks better due to its cool moisture activated properties.They're perfect for irritation caused by braces brackets or dental wax for Invisalign trays.

items.title: Platypus Orthodontic Flossers for Braces
items.text: One of the reasons why this is a patient fav- its quick and easy to use! The spatula end of this flosser for braces simply fits under your arch wire so you can floss like normal in between brackets. Great for on-the-go and after meals. You can floss your entire mouth in less than 2 minutes!

items.title: Plackers Grind No More Night Guard, Nighttime Protection for Teeth
items.text: Plackers dental night guard provides a comfortable option for patients who grind who do not have a traditional night guard that they are using. A nice feature that patients appreciate with this option is it is ready to wear with no boiling, cutting or molding required.

image.alt: Plackers Grind No More Night Guard, Nighttime Protection for Teeth

items.title: Shock Doctor Orthodontic Mouth Guard for Braces for Sports
items.text: Another braces must for our athletes with braces. Shock doctor mouth guard are designed to fit comfortably over orthodontic brackets, offering maximum comfort as a contact sports mouth guard for braces during active play

image.alt: Shock Doctor Orthodontic Mouth Guard for Braces for Sports

items.title: 11Philips Sonicare
items.text: It has been shown that electric tooth brushes are more effective at plaque removal, removing up to 7x more plaque than a manual toothbrush while protecting your gums. Dr. Rocha loves the timer to help patients brush at least 2 minutes and has a cool feature called "smart brushing" which alerts you when you are using too much pressure while brushing (which our gums do not like!). Oral B also has a great option similar to this model.

items.title: Waterpik
items.text: Waterpik water flossers are a great additional to your hygiene routine, as they deliver a combination of water pressure and pulsations, removing up to 99.9 percent of plaque bacteria from treated areas that cause gingivitis, cavities, and bad breath. It is not a replacement for floss, but it is great at giving the mouth a "power wash" and flushing food/plaque out of those hard to reach areas.

items.title: Clip-on Comfort Covers for Braces (wax alternative)
items.text: This snazzy item is an alternative to the traditional wax, and a favorite amongst athletes, musicians, and patients early in treatment as they adjust to the braces and wires. The cover provides an extra layer of relief to minimize the pain and irritation caused by sharp braces and rough wires.

image.alt: Clip-on Comfort Covers for Braces (wax alternative)

items.title: Superfloss
items.text: Patients love this item because it has this stiff end that makes it easier to floss under wires and appliances.

items.title: Floss threader
items.text: Another floss option that pairs with floss and helps get under the wires and in between teeth. Great for both braces patients and patients with bonded "permanent" retainers.

items.title: Orthodontic Travel Kit
items.text: All the helpful items for our braces patients in one! Great item to have on hand when traveling, includes a travel tooth brush, wax, floss and floss threader, a little mirror and timer to use when brushing!

items.title: Aligner Chewies
items.text: The ultimate tool to pair with your Invisalign treatment. They help seat the aligner to ensure it is properly moving your teeth. We recommend keeping these nifty tools in your Invisalign/retainer case so you can use them each time you put your aligners on!

items.title: L'il Palates Sweepers
items.text: This helpful tool helps remove food trapped between a fixed orthodontic appliance (palatal expanders, MARA, Herbst, etc.) and gums, palate and teeth. It is gentle to the mouth, easy to use, and folds up into a convenient case with a keychain that patients can bring to school, weekend trips, etc!

image.alt: Close up of braces for orthodontic first aid guidance

paragraphs.text: True orthodontic emergencies are rare, but when they occur, we are available for you. As a general rule, you should call the office when you experience severe pain or when you have a painful appliance problem that you cannot take care of yourself. When the office is closed, Dr. Valley and her assistants are on call and can be reached on their cell phone. If Dr. Valley is out of town, she has a local orthodontist who will be available to take care of your emergency needs.

You might be surprised to learn that you may be able to temporarily solve many problems yourself. If you are able to alleviate your discomfort on your own, it is very important that you still call our office to inform us of the problem and to schedule an appointment for us to assess the situation.

If you have a damaged appliance, but it is not causing you discomfort, we advise you still call our office to inform us of the situation, so that we may schedule a regular appointment with you to take care of your appliance. Allowing your appliance to remain damaged for an extended period of time may result in disruptions in your treatment plan.

image.alt: Patient with braces discomfort using orthodontic first aid

braces-first-aid.title: If you have a poking wire...

paragraphs.text:
If you have a poky wire, don’t worry — it’s a common and easy-to-manage issue! 😊 Here’s what you can do at home:

✔ Use orthodontic wax
Dry the area with a tissue and place a small piece of wax over the poky end to protect your cheek or gums.

✔ Trim it carefully (if needed)
If the wire is sticking out in the back and you can clearly see it, you may use a clean nail clipper to gently clip the very end. Make sure you can reach it safely and avoid swallowing the piece.

✔ Push it back into place
Sometimes you can use the eraser end of a pencil or a clean cotton swab to gently tuck the wire back against the bracket.

✔ Rinse with warm salt water
If the area is irritated, rinse with warm salt water to soothe soreness.

If the wire continues to bother you or you’re unsure about trimming it, please contact our office — we’re happy to help and can schedule a quick comfort visit if needed!

image.alt: If you have a poking wire...

braces-first-aid.title: If a brace comes loose from a tooth...

paragraphs.text:
What should I do if a bracket breaks?

Don’t worry — it happens sometimes! 😊 Here’s what to do:

✔ Save the bracket
If it has completely come off, please keep it and bring it with you to your next appointment.

✔ Leave it in place if still attached
If the bracket is still connected to the wire but loose, leave it where it is.

✔ Use orthodontic wax
Place wax over the loose bracket to prevent irritation to your cheeks or gums.

✔ Avoid hard or sticky foods
This will help prevent further damage until we can repair it.

Please give our office a call so we can let you know if you need a quick repair visit or if it can wait until your next scheduled appointment. We’re happy to help!

image.alt: If a brace comes loose from a tooth...

braces-first-aid.title: If the main wire has come out of the brace or tube on a back molar tooth...

paragraphs.text: Attempt to reinsert the wire with a pair of needle nosed pliers or tweezers. If the wire is not sticking, place a piece of wax over the area. If the wire is sticking and wax does not help, the wire can be cut with a small wire cutter or nail clipper close to the back of the last brace. This is a last resort if professional help is unavailable.

image.alt: If the main wire has come out of the brace or tube on a back molar tooth...

braces-first-aid.title: If your teeth become sore after a visit...

paragraphs.text: We generally recommend that you take an analgesic such as acetaminophen (e.g., Tylenol) or ibuprofen (e.g., Advil, Motrin). This, of course, is the protocol only if there are no known allergies to these medications. Rinsing with Peroxyl or a cup of warm water and teaspoon of salt can also be soothing. Chewing sugarless gum for 10 to 15 minutes following an adjustment can often reduce the soreness that may develop.

image.alt: If your teeth become sore after a visit...

braces-first-aid.title: If your lips, cheeks and tongue have become irritated...

paragraphs.text: This is normal for one to two weeks as they toughen and become accustomed to the surface of the braces. You can place a small ball of wax on the braces to smooth the area. If a small sore has developed on the inside of your cheek or lip, use Orabase or a similar non-prescription topical anesthetic that is available at your local drug store.

image.alt: If your lips, cheeks and tongue have become irritated...

braces-first-aid.title: If a part of your braces or your retainer breaks...

paragraphs.text: Save the piece and call to set up a repair visit. If your appliance is not repairable, it may be necessary to fabricate a replacement.

image.alt: If a part of your braces or your retainer breaks...

braces-first-aid.title: If you notice some small spaces developing between your teeth while you have braces on...

paragraphs.text: Don't panic! This is usually normal and expected as teeth move into different positions, particularly in the early stages of treatment. They will be closed eventually.

image.alt: If you notice some small spaces developing between your teeth while you have braces on...

braces-first-aid.title: If a rubber spacer falls out...

paragraphs.text: You can try to replace it your self by threading a couple pieces of floss through it and then pulling it under and up between the teeth. Then remove the floss. However, for most it is best to just call the office to schedule an appointment to have us replace it. It only takes us 5 minutes or less.

image.alt: If a rubber spacer falls out...

braces-first-aid.title: If you experience any bleeding when brushing or flossing...

paragraphs.text: That usually means there is a local area of irritation, possibly from retained plaque or food. Continue to keep the area clean by brushing, flossing and rinsing with Listerine.

image.alt: If you experience any bleeding when brushing or flossing...

braces-first-aid.title: If a particular area is painful or you notice a bump of any sort...

paragraphs.text:
We’re here to help! 😊 Mild soreness can happen with orthodontic treatment, but we’d like to know about anything unusual.

✔ Check for irritation
Look to see if a wire or bracket may be rubbing the area. You can place orthodontic wax over anything that feels sharp or irritating.

✔ Rinse with warm salt water
This can help soothe tender spots and reduce inflammation.

✔ Take a photo
Please send our office a clear picture of the area so we can better understand what’s going on and guide you on next steps.

✔ Call us if discomfort continues
If the pain increases, swelling develops, or you’re unsure what to do, contact our office. We’ll let you know if you should come in for a quick visit.

We want you to feel comfortable and confident during treatment — don’t hesitate to reach out!

image.alt: If a particular area is painful or you notice a bump of any sort...

paragraphs.text: Valley Orthodontics believes that creating a beautiful smile involves more than just straightening teeth, so we look at the whole face when treating our patients. Our unique approach to orthodontics involves non-extraction treatment to achieve beautifully balanced faces, full smiles and healthy bites. We take pride in our work and use only the highest quality appliances and the latest technology to create your perfect smile!
The Most Common Type of Braces. Today’s metal braces are much smaller, flatter, and more comfortable than in the past. Patients of all ages choose metal braces to help them.
Many of today’s braces are made from either clear or natural tooth-colored materials to provide patients with an attractive, less noticeable alternative to traditional braces.
There’s never been a better time to straighten your teeth with the most advanced clear aligner in the world. Our office is proud to be a certified provider of Invisalign clear aligners.
Surgical orthodontics, also known as orthognathic surgery, is a type of orthodontic treatment used to correct severe cases that include bad bites, jaw bone abnormalities, and malocclusion.
Carriere Self-Ligating Bracket System has been created to give you an amazing smile with maximum comfort and a reduction in treatment length by an average of four months.
Clear aligners are an effective alternative to traditional braces because they use a system of clear, removable trays used to straighten teeth. Each aligner is custom-made to fit over your teeth.
Wearing elastics improves the fit of your upper and lower teeth. Wear rubber bands as instructed, and remember that work far more efficiently if they’re worn as prescribed.
The palatal expander expands (or widens) your upper jaw by putting gentle pressure on your upper molars each time an adjustment is made.

paragraphs.text: The benefits of having a beautiful confident smile that can last a lifetime are truly priceless. Orthodontic treatment is an excellent investment in your overall dental, physical and emotional health. Because each case is unique, each patient’s treatment fee is determined on an individual basis with difficulty and length of treatment being taken into consideration. Being sensitive to the fact that different people have different needs in fulfilling their financial obligations, we offer several options for payment.

These include interest-free monthly payment plans via automatic monthly bank or credit card drafts. We also offer extended financing options that can make your monthly payments more comfortable, credit for pre-payment in full, consideration for multiple family members in treatment at the same time, assistance with filing your insurance claims and help with flexible spending accounts. We will do everything that we can to make your treatment with our office convenient and manageable. If you have any questions or special requests regarding any financial matter, please give us a call!
We’re Here to Help!

paragraphs.text: Our greatest goal is to give you the healthy, straight, beautiful smile that you’ve always wanted for yourself or for your child. We understand that choosing the right orthodontic care is a big decision, and we respect your reasons for wanting to improve the way you look and feel. Our practice will work with you every step of the way to make sure your orthodontic care is as rewarding as possible. Our practice utilized the latest technology, offering a variety of safe and gentle treatment options to give you and your child the customized, individual care you deserve.

In an effort to make your first visit as informative and efficient as possible, we invite you to complete the new patient forms. We will send the forms to you by mail, or you have the option of downloading them from our website. Please bring these completed forms to your appointment.
At your first visit you will have the opportunity to meet Dr. Valley and our team, learn more about orthodontics and what this means for you or your child. During this initial visit, we will:

• Review your new patient forms, including your dental and medical history
  • Take photographs
  • Provide a complete oral exam
  • Determine whether orthodontic treatment is beneficial
  • Discuss various treatment options
  • Create a customized treatment plan, including the appropriate time to begin treatment and also the estimated length of treatment
  • Discuss all financial information, insurance benefits and payment plans
  • Answer any questions you may have

tabs.header: What Happens After Your Consultation Visit?

paragraphs.text: The next step after your consultation appointment is to get standard orthodontic records. These consist of x-rays, photographs and impressions or a 3D scan of your teeth. From these records, Dr. Valley will be able to put together your most appropriate and detailed orthdontic treatment plan. All records and a full report will be sent to your dentist. We also recommend that you get a complete dental examination and cleaning at your dentist’s office so that we know your teeth and gums are healthy and no restorative work is required. Once these steps are completed, you will get started on achieving the smile you’ve always wanted!

Visiting the orthodontist about every four to eight weeks is an important part of your orthodontic treatment. These appointments can vary, but are approximately 30 minutes long. We understand that life is busy and will work with you to make sure your appointments are scheduled as conveniently as possible for you.

tabs.header: Visiting your Dentist during Orthodontic Treatment

paragraphs.text: It is very important that you continue to visit your dentist once every six months, even during your orthodontic treatment, for teeth cleanings and routine dental checkups. If extra dental care is needed, we will be happy to coordinate with your family dentist to make sure that you are receiving the best care possible.

what-to-expect-on-the-first-day-of-brace.title: What to Expect on the First Day of Braces

paragraphs.text: The appointment to place braces is usually 1.5-2.5 hours long. We request that this appointment be scheduled in the morning when you or your child is most alert and less tired. This is also a time of the day when the office is quieter, as opposed to after school hours. After this appointment you or your child should be well enough to return to work or school for the remainder of the day.

what-to-expect-on-the-first-day-of-brace.title: What to Expect on the First Day of Braces Guide

paragraphs.text: The bonding adhesives used to attach braces take approximately 24 hours to reach maximum strength. Therefore, it is important not to eat hard or crunchy food immediately (or during the duration of your treatment) or the braces may come off. Also, as your teeth begin to move and become straight, you will find that a soft diet for the first three days is most comfortable. At your first appointment, we will provide you with recommendations for foods to avoid and suggestions for foods to prepare to protect your braces and teeth.

Mild tooth discomfort may begin a few hours after the braces are placed and last (on average) up to three days. Ibuprofen is the analgesic medication of choice (Advil or Motrin). If you feel you or your child may benefit from Ibuprofen, take it 30 minutes before the appointment as prescribed. Every person’s pain threshold is different, so please use your discretion and follow pain management as directed by your physician or your child’s pediatrician.

paragraphs.text: Invisalign® is one of the most modern and popular treatment options for straightening teeth. Using custom-made series of aligners, your teeth are gradually and gently shifted into their desired location. These trays eliminate the need for metal brackets and wires that traditional braces offer. With Invisalign®, all you have to do is wear your trays for 20-22 hours a day and change them once every two weeks until your treatment is finished. This allows you to achieve your dream smile with little disruption to your daily life. Because these trays are removable, you can continue to eat your favorite foods and brush and floss normally. The best part? Invisalign® is virtually invisible so most people don’t even realize you’re undergoing treatment!

image.alt: doctor with patient in orthodontic setting

accordion.header: Is Dr. Rocha Experienced With the Invisalign System?

paragraphs.text: Our office is proud to announce that Dr. Rocha has extensive experience treating patients of all ages, starting as young as 7 years old! Dr. Rocha attended a rigorous post graduate residency program that has one of the strongest Invisalign programs, being taught under orthodontists who helped create Invisalign! Dr. Rocha introduced the Itero 3-D digital model scanner to Valley orthodontics, which will allow in office scans of patients teeth. No more messy, uncomfortable dental impressions or visits to the lab for digital scans!

image.alt: doctor with patient in orthodontic setting

paragraphs.text:
Invisalign utilizes 3-D computer imaging technology to transform impressions of your teeth into a series of customized plastic aligners.

Each aligner is worn for approximately two weeks. They are removed only to eat, brush, and floss.

As you replace each aligner with the next, your teeth will move – little by little, week by week – until they reach the final alignment that Dr. Rocha has prescribed.

The number of aligners you wear depends on the severity of crowding or spacing you have.

Invisalign has been proven effective for adults, teenagers, and even children in clinical research and practices nationwide. Dr. Rocha can determine if this treatment is an effective option for you. You can obtain more information at Invisalign’s website, www.invisalign.com.

image.alt: doctor with patient in orthodontic setting

paragraphs.text: At Valley Orthodontics, Invisalign costs the same as traditional braces. We want to help our patients choose the treatment that is right for them.

image.alt: doctor with patient in orthodontic setting

accordion.header: What Do I Need to Get Started?

paragraphs.text: At your inial appointment we will discuss your concerns and determine a treatment plan that is right for you. Next, an appointment for your 3D Invisalign scan can be scheduled as soon as possible. After the 3D scan of your teeth is taken, the file is sent to Invisalign. Working with Invisalign, Dr. Valley determines how your finished result will be accomplished. Once the treatment plan is finalized, the aligners are fabricated and shipped and we can get started.

image.alt: doctor with patient in orthodontic setting

accordion.header: How Often Will I Need to Be Seen For Appointments?

paragraphs.text:
Each aligner tray set is worn for approximately two weeks. Dr. Rocha will give you 3 to 4 sets at a time, so generally appointments are every 6 to 8 weeks.

Consider Invisalign to get the beautiful straight teeth you’ve always wanted – without braces. A consultation with Dr. Rocha can determine if Invisalign is right for you. Contact us for your Invisalign consultation.

paragraphs.text: DDS, MSD Specialist in Orthodontics for children and adults

Dr. Rocha is a bay area native; where she has been practicing orthodontics for the past eight years. She was the first in her family to pursue higher education, starting at UCSD where she received the highest GPA in her major. She then received her Doctor of Dental Surgery degree at University of California, San Francisco. Dr. Rocha was then one of eight residents admitted to University of the Pacific, where she received a masters degree and in depth training in orthodontics. Dr. Rocha received numerous awards over the course of her many years in college and graduate school.

Dr. Rocha also uses her expertise to enrich philanthropy; in one instance, Dr. Rocha traveled to Honduras to provide free dental treatment to the local underserved communities. She loves the opportunity to participate in local health fairs and any opportunity to mentor and connect to the community.

Dr. Rocha began her orthodontic career only a week after graduating and she hasn't stopped since. ("The longer I practice, the more I fall in love with what I do!") From her years in training and practice, Dr. Rocha is a leader in her field in treating patients of all ages and using both braces and clear aligners. Her favorite part of the job is building long term relationships with her patients and coworkers.

Dr. Rocha believes every patient has individual needs, and their orthodontic plan and care should reflect that. Orthodontics is more than a beautiful smile- having your teeth in a healthy position sets a patient up for a lifetime of improved dental health.

When she's not creating beautiful smiles at the office, Dr. Rocha enjoys exploring local parks with her husband and their adventurous, energetic children- Jayden and Maya. In her rare moments of kid-free time, she loves running half marathons with her sisters, diving into audiobooks or true crime podcasts, and savoring new culinary spots with friends and family.
Dr. Rocha is a member of the American Dental Association, California Dental Association, and the Marin County Dental Society.

paragraphs.text: ​Dr. Rocha and her team have the knowledge and experience to give you the smile that you’ve always dreamed about! Join the many families who have entrusted their smiles to Dr. Rocha, who has been creating smiles for over 10 years!

Dr. Rocha is on the forefront of the latest technology, having introduced the 3-D Itero digital scanner to Valley Orthodontics. Our team consists of experienced and certified business and dental assistants, also with years of experience in orthodontics.
The Magic Wand: Fast, Needle-Free Pain Relief

At Valley Orthodontics, we’re always looking for ways to make your smile journey more comfortable—and our Magic Wand does just that. Also known as the Dental Pain Eraser, this innovative handheld device uses safe, low-level electronic pulses to interrupt pain signals, offering fast relief from soreness caused by braces or aligners.

We use the Magic Wand right here in our orthodontic offices across the Valley, especially after adjustments or the placement of new trays. It’s gentle, non-invasive, and can even be used at home with guidance from our team. Simply glide the device along the gumline for a few seconds per area—most patients experience noticeable relief within minutes.

Whether you’re from San Rafael or the surrounding Bay Area, Valley Orthodontics is proud to offer the latest innovations to support your smile journey. Comfort, care, and advanced tools like this one are just part of what makes our approach truly unique.

traveling-with-invisalign-airports-eatin.title: Traveling With Invisalign: Airports, Eating Out, and Keeping Track of Aligners

image.alt: Traveling With Invisalign: Airports, Eating Out, and Keeping Track of Aligners at Valley Orthodontics

traveling-with-invisalign-airports-eatin.title: Traveling With Invisalign: Airports, Eating Out, and Keeping Track of Aligners

paragraphs.text:
Traveling is exciting — but if you’re wearing Invisalign aligners, you may wonder how to keep your treatment on track while you’re away from home. The good news? Invisalign is one of the easiest orthodontic treatments to travel with. With a little preparation and a few smart habits, you can enjoy your vacation, business trip, or weekend getaway without stressing about your smile.

At Valley Orthodontics, we help Invisalign patients throughout Marin County stay confident and comfortable during treatment — whether they live in San Rafael, Mill Valley, Larkspur, Corte Madera, Novato, or surrounding communities.

Pack an Invisalign Travel Kit

Before you leave home, put together a small Invisalign travel kit you can keep in your purse, backpack, or carry-on bag. This can save you from scrambling in an airport restroom or restaurant bathroom later.

Your Invisalign travel kit should include:

  • Your aligner case
  • A toothbrush and travel-size toothpaste
  • Floss or floss picks
  • Cleaning crystals or aligner cleaner
  • Extra chewies if you use them
  • Lip balm (travel can dry out your lips)
  • Your next set of trays, if you’ll switch during your trip
  • Your previous set of aligners as a backup

Keeping everything in one place helps you stay organized and avoid losing trays while traveling.

Invisalign Airport Tips for Travelers

Airports can be hectic, especially during early morning flights or long layovers. These Invisalign travel tips can help make flying with aligners much easier.

Keep Aligners in Your Carry-On

Never pack your aligners in checked luggage. If your suitcase gets delayed or lost, your trays could disappear with it. Always keep your current aligners — and ideally your next set — in your carry-on bag.

Stay Hydrated During Flights

Flying can dry out your mouth, which may make your aligners feel tighter or less comfortable. Drink plenty of water throughout your flight. Water is also the safest drink to enjoy while wearing Invisalign aligners.

Never Wrap Aligners in Napkins

One of the most common ways people lose Invisalign trays while traveling is by wrapping them in a napkin during meals. At airports, restaurants, and on planes, it’s easy to accidentally throw them away. Always use your aligner case instead.

Set Phone Reminders

Travel changes routines. Between sightseeing, family visits, and changing time zones, it’s easy to forget when to put your aligners back in. Phone alarms or Invisalign tracking apps can help you stay close to the recommended 20–22 hours of wear per day.

Eating Out With Invisalign Aligners

One of the biggest advantages of Invisalign treatment is the freedom to eat the foods you love. But restaurants and travel dining still require a little planning.

Remove Aligners Before Eating

Always take your trays out before meals or snacks. Eating with aligners in place can damage or stain them.

Rinse Before Reinserting

After eating, brush your teeth before putting your aligners back in whenever possible. If brushing isn’t available, rinsing your mouth and aligners with water is much better than nothing until you can clean your teeth properly.

Be Careful With Coffee and Colored Drinks

Hot coffee, tea, red wine, and dark sodas can stain clear aligners. If you want to enjoy these drinks, remove your trays first whenever possible.

Keep Your Invisalign Case Nearby

Restaurant tables are another place where aligners often go missing. Keeping your aligner case easily accessible can help prevent accidental loss.

How to Keep Track of Invisalign Trays While Traveling

Losing aligners during a trip can be frustrating, but a few simple habits can make it much less likely.

Always Use the Same Pocket or Bag

Choose one dedicated spot for your aligner case every day. Consistency makes it easier to remember where your trays are.

Bring Your Previous Set of Aligners

If your current trays are lost or damaged, your previous aligners can temporarily help prevent your teeth from shifting until you contact your orthodontist.

Label Your Aligner Case

Adding your name and phone number to your aligner case may help if it gets left behind in a hotel, airport lounge, or restaurant.

Avoid Leaving Aligners in Hot Cars

Heat can warp Invisalign trays and affect how they fit. Avoid leaving aligners in parked cars, direct sunlight, or near hot surfaces.

What to Do If You Lose Invisalign Aligners While Traveling

If you lose an aligner during your trip, don’t panic.

  • Contact your orthodontic office for guidance
  • Wear your previous set if instructed
  • Avoid going without aligners for long periods
  • Continue wearing retainers or backup trays if available

At Valley Orthodontics, we help Invisalign patients throughout San Rafael, Mill Valley, Larkspur, Corte Madera, Novato, and nearby Marin County communities navigate unexpected situations during treatment, including lost or damaged trays while traveling.

Quick Invisalign Travel Checklist

Before leaving for your trip, make sure you:

  • ✔ Pack your aligner case
  • ✔ Bring toothbrush and toothpaste
  • ✔ Carry your next tray set
  • ✔ Pack previous trays as backup
  • ✔ Set wear-time reminders
  • ✔ Keep aligners in your carry-on bag
  • ✔ Bring aligner cleaner or cleaning crystals

Why Invisalign Is Popular Among Busy Marin County Patients

Many patients choose Invisalign because it fits easily into active lifestyles, busy work schedules, and travel plans. Whether you’re commuting, traveling for business, or planning a family vacation, Invisalign aligners offer flexibility and convenience without the dietary restrictions often associated with traditional braces.

At Valley Orthodontics, we proudly provide Invisalign treatment for patients in San Rafael, Mill Valley, Larkspur, Corte Madera, Novato, and surrounding Marin County communities, helping teens and adults achieve healthy, confident smiles with personalized orthodontic care.

Travel With Confidence During Invisalign Treatment

Traveling with Invisalign gets easier very quickly. Once you build a few good habits, caring for your aligners becomes just another part of your routine — like packing your phone charger or bringing your toothbrush.

The most important thing is consistency. Wearing your aligners as directed, keeping them clean, and storing them safely will help keep your treatment moving forward no matter where life takes you.

Whether you’re heading out on a family vacation, school trip, or weekend getaway, Invisalign makes it possible to travel comfortably while still working toward a healthier, straighter smile.

If you’re considering Invisalign treatment in San Rafael, Mill Valley, Larkspur, Corte Madera, Novato, or nearby areas, the team at Valley Orthodontics is here to help you achieve a confident, healthy smile with expert orthodontic care tailored to your lifestyle.

Common Myths About Orthodontic Treatment Debunked

If you’ve ever considered orthodontic treatment for yourself or your child, you’ve probably heard a few myths along the way. From “braces are only for kids” to “treatment takes forever,” there’s a lot of outdated information floating around online and through word of mouth.

At Valley Orthodontics, we believe patients and families in San Rafael and throughout Marin County deserve clear, honest information about their orthodontic options. Modern orthodontics is more comfortable, convenient, and effective than ever before.

Let’s take a look at some of the most common myths about orthodontic treatment — and the truth behind them.

Myth #1: Braces Are Only for Kids and Teenagers

One of the biggest misconceptions about orthodontics is that treatment is only for younger patients.

The Truth:

Adults make up a growing percentage of orthodontic patients today. Many people choose treatment later in life to improve their smile, bite, oral health, or confidence.

Modern options like clear braces and Invisalign aligners make orthodontic treatment more discreet and flexible for busy adults and professionals.

Whether you’re 7 or 87, healthy teeth can often be moved successfully at almost any age.

Myth #2: Orthodontic Treatment Is Only Cosmetic

It’s easy to think braces are just about achieving a straighter smile.

Orthodontic treatment also improve oral health and function. Correcting bite issues and alignment problems may help:

  • Make teeth easier to clean
  • Reduce uneven tooth wear
  • Improve chewing and speaking
  • Lower the risk of gum problems
  • Help relieve strain on jaw joints

A healthy bite is about much more than appearance. As Dr. Rocha explains, the focus of orthodontics is to move your teeth to the healthiest possible position so you can keep your teeth for life. The nice side effect is your smile will look great at the end too!

Myth #3: Braces Are Extremely Painful

Many patients worry that braces will hurt constantly.

Orthodontic treatment has become much more comfortable over the years. Patients may experience mild soreness for a few days after adjustments or switching aligners, but severe pain is uncommon.

Today’s orthodontic technology uses lighter, gentler forces designed to move teeth more efficiently and comfortably. Most patients adjust very quickly to braces or aligners.

And special bonus for our Valley Orthodontic patients- their treatment just became even MORE comfortable with our special “magic wand” device. This special gadget, which involves no medication or needles to work, helps relieve soreness for 2 hours up to 2 days. Ask about the magic wand at your next visit!

Myth #4: Treatment Takes Many Years

Some people avoid orthodontics because they assume they’ll be in braces forever.

Every smile is different, but many treatments are completed in 12–24 months. Some minor corrections may even take less time.

Advancements in orthodontic technology have made treatment more efficient than ever before. Following instructions carefully — especially with elastic wear or aligner use — can also help keep treatment on schedule.

Myth #5: Invisalign/Aligners Only Works for Minor Problems

Many people believe clear aligners can only fix small gaps or slightly crooked teeth.

Invisalign can treat many orthodontic concerns, including:

  • Crowding
  • Spacing
  • Overbites
  • Underbites
  • Crossbites
  • Open bites

Not every patient is a candidate for aligners, but Clear Aligner technology has advanced significantly and can often treat more complex cases than people realize. Dr. Rocha has extensive experience with aligners and proudly treats patients of all ages, including complex cases.

Myth #6: You Don’t Need Retainers After Treatment

A common misconception is that once braces come off, treatment is finished forever.

Retainers are an important part of protecting your new smile. Teeth naturally shift over time, even after orthodontic treatment.

Wearing retainers as directed helps maintain the results you worked hard to achieve. And retainer wear is for life- or as long as you want to keep your teeth straight 🙂

Think of retainers as the “maintenance plan” for your smile.

Myth #7: Orthodontic Treatment Is Purely About Appearance

Some families worry treatment is optional unless teeth look severely crooked.

Orthodontic evaluations can identify bite and alignment issues before they become bigger concerns. Early monitoring may help guide jaw development, create space for incoming teeth, and reduce future complications.

In many cases, orthodontic care supports both long-term dental health and confidence.

The Bottom Line

Orthodontic treatment has come a long way. Today’s options are more comfortable, more discreet, and more personalized than many people expect.

The best way to separate myths from facts is to schedule a professional evaluation and ask questions about your specific smile goals and concerns.

At Valley Orthodontics, we’re proud to help families throughout San Rafael and Marin County feel informed and confident about their orthodontic care.

More Than Just a Straight Smile: Why Airway Matters in Orthodontics

When you think of the orthodontist, you probably think of braces, clear aligners, and perfectly straight teeth. While a beautiful smile is a fantastic goal, at Valley Orthodontics, we believe it’s only one piece of the puzzle.

As an airway-centered orthodontic practice, we don’t just look at your teeth; we look at the person behind the smile. Our goal is to identify the root cause of dental issues, ensuring our patients breathe, sleep, and live better.

Why Your Airway is Part of Your Orthodontic Plan

Have you ever wondered why teeth become crowded in the first place? Often, it’s because the “housing” for the teeth—the dental arches—is too narrow or constricted. When the mouth is narrow, the airway is often impacted as well.

By evaluating the airway, we can determine if a patient is struggling to breathe properly through their nose. Proper nasal breathing is essential for:

  • Facial Development: Helping the jaw grow into its correct position.
  • Quality Sleep: Ensuring the brain and body get enough oxygen at night.
  • Overall Health: Reducing the risk of long-term issues like sleep apnea.

Signs Your Child Might Need an Airway Evaluation

Sometimes, the symptoms of a restricted airway aren’t obvious. If your child experiences any of the following, it might be time for a holistic orthodontic check-up:

  • Snoring or heavy breathing during sleep.
  • Grinding teeth (often a sign the body is trying to “wake up” the airway).
  • Restless sleep or waking up tired.
  • Difficulty concentrating or behavioral issues at school (often mistaken for ADHD).
  • Mouth breathing during the day or night.

High-Tech Diagnosis for Holistic Care

We’ve invested in the latest technology to ensure we see the full picture. At our San Rafael office, we use:

  • 3D Imaging (CBCT): Unlike traditional 2D X-rays, our 3D scans allow us to visualize the airway, nasal passages, and jaw structure in incredible detail. We can literally see if there is an obstruction in the nose or throat.
  • Digital Scans: No more messy impressions! We use precise digital scanners to create a 3D model of the teeth and arches, helping us plan how to expand narrow arches to create more room for both teeth and air.

Treating the Source, Not Just the Symptom

Our philosophy is simple: We treat the source of the problem. If a patient has a narrow arch or a restricted airway, simply straightening the teeth won’t fix the underlying health issue.

By diagnosing these issues early, we can:

  • Expand the palate to improve airflow and create space for teeth.
  • Collaborate with Specialists: We work closely with a network of experts, including ENTs (Ear, Nose, and Throat doctors) , Allergists, and Myofunctional Therapists to ensure you get the comprehensive care you deserve.

Start Your Journey at Valley Orthodontics

Located conveniently in San Rafael, Marin County, we are proud to serve our community with a health-first approach to orthodontics. Whether you’re interested in braces for your child or a more comfortable night’s sleep for yourself, we are here to help.

Ready to breathe easier and smile brighter?

7 Important Questions Parents Should Ask at an Orthodontic Consultation

If you’re a parent searching for a trusted orthodontist near you, you may be wondering when your child should see an orthodontist and how to prepare for the first visit. An orthodontic consultation is an important step in protecting your child’s long-term oral health—and it should feel informative, comfortable, and stress-free.

At Valley Orthodontics in San Rafael, we believe that educated parents make confident decisions. Below are seven essential questions parents should ask at an orthodontic consultation to help determine the best care for their child’s smile.

💡 Download our helpful guide: Click here to download the Parent Consultation Checklist (PDF)

1. Does My Child Need Braces or Orthodontic Treatment Right Now?

One of the most common questions parents ask is, “Does my child need braces yet? When is the best time to schedule an initial consultation?” The answer isn’t always immediate treatment. In many cases, early orthodontic evaluations allow us to monitor growth and recommend treatment at the ideal time. We recommend patients visiting an orthodontist for an initial screening no later than 7 years old, earlier if concerns exist from you or their dentist.

A trusted San Rafael orthodontist will explain whether treatment should begin now, later, or if observation is the best approach based on your child’s development. And equally important, ask why now is or use the time, if an explanation isn’t offered.

2. What Type of Orthodontic Treatment Is Best for My Child?

There are several orthodontic treatment options for children and teens, including traditional braces, clear braces, and clear aligners. The best option depends on your child’s bite, jaw growth, tooth alignment, and lifestyle.

Ask your orthodontist:

  • Which treatment option is recommended
  • If a particular option is better suited for any major sport/hobbies/lifestyle (mention if your an athlete, play an instrument, etc, in case a treatment option is best suited)
  • Pros and cons of each treatment option, including compliance required for success

At Valley Orthodontics, we focus on personalized treatment plans—because every smile and every child is different. Ask about our exclusive “champagne” colored brackets- a local favorite of patients of all ages!

3. How Long Will Orthodontic Treatment Take?

Many parents search online for “how long do braces take for kids?” While treatment time varies, your orthodontist should provide a realistic estimate and explain what factors may influence the timeline, including growth patterns, oral hygiene, and compliance.

Clear expectations help families plan with confidence.

4. How Much Do Braces Cost and What’s Included?

Orthodontic treatment is an investment in you and/or your child’s health and confidence. Be sure to ask for a clear explanation of:

  • Total treatment cost
  • What’s included (adjustments, retainers, repairs, emergency visits)
  • Insurance coverage and flexible payment options

A reputable Marin County orthodontic office will always be transparent and happy to review financial details with you.

5. How Often Are Orthodontic Appointments?

Knowing how frequently appointments are scheduled helps families balance school, work, and extracurricular activities. Ask how often your child will need to come in and how flexible the office is if appointments need to be rescheduled.

Convenient, family-friendly care makes orthodontic treatment much easier for parents and kids alike.

6. What Can We Do at Home to Support Treatment?

Successful orthodontic treatment is a team effort. Ask your orthodontist about:

  • Proper brushing and flossing with braces or aligners
  • Foods to avoid during treatment
  • How to manage soreness or minor issues at home

Following home care instructions helps keep treatment on track and avoid broken appliances.

7. What Happens After Braces Come Off?

Many parents don’t realize how important retainers are after orthodontic treatment. Ask about:

  • Retainer options
  • How long retainers are needed
  • Long-term follow-up care
  • Cost of retainers and how open they need to be replaced

Protecting your child’s new smile is just as important as creating it.

Why Parents Trust Valley Orthodontics

Choosing an orthodontist is about more than straight teeth—it’s about trust, communication, and long-term care. At Valley Orthodontics, we take the time to answer questions, explain treatment options clearly, and guide families through every stage of orthodontic care. And welcoming you to the Valley Ortho Family 🙂

If you’re searching for a trusted orthodontist for children and teens in San Rafael or Marin County, we’re here to help.

Comprehensive Orthodontic Care for Kids & Families

Early orthodontic care plays a vital role. It shapes a child’s long-term oral health, facial development, and overall confidence. On-time detection and treatment of alignment problems like bite irregularities and spacing issues can help prevent numerous complications in the future.

Thankfully, modern orthodontics has come a long way in recent years. It offers gentle, efficient, and highly precise treatment options for kids, teens, and adults. Plus, digital tools, customized appliances, and technology-driven planning ensure effective orthodontic care tailored to their needs.

Why Early Orthodontic Care Matters

The American Association of Orthodontists suggests that orthodontic assessments should be conducted on children at around age seven. Specialists can identify crowding, crossbites, open bites, and jaw growth discrepancies at this age.

Early assessment lets orthodontists treat at the appropriate developmental phase. It helps them guide the development of teeth.

What Makes a Great Orthodontist for Kids

An expert pediatric orthodontist combines years of practice with patience and gentleness. They can explain treatments in ways that keep both children and parents informed and comfortable.

Digital scanning, low-radiation imaging, and modern monitoring devices ensure faster and stress-free procedures. Above all, a fantastic kids’ orthodontist develops trust during the initial stages that makes every visit positive and reassuring.

Orthodontic Treatments for Kids and Teens

Children and teens benefit from a range of treatment options. These include:

  • Traditional metal braces
  • Ceramic braces
  • Clear aligners

Orthodontists tailor treatment plans according to growth patterns, tooth development, lifestyle, and comfort.

Expert Orthodontic Care for All Ages

Although early treatment is very important, orthodontic solutions are now equally developed for both teens and adults. Digital planning, 3-D printing, and finely designed appliances deliver effective and accurate results at any age.

Adults particularly embrace discreet treatment options. These include clear aligners and low-profile brackets that blend well into daily lives. These modern tools allow orthodontists to deliver transformative results with minimal disruption to professional and social life.

Emergency Orthodontic Care – What You Should Know

Broken brackets, poking wires, loose bands, or lost retainers may be considered orthodontic emergencies. A timely response prevents unnecessary discomfort. It ensures appliances continue functioning correctly. Experienced orthodontists provide fast, compassionate care. That’s how they resolve urgent issues and help patients get back on track without stress.

The Role of Expertise in Orthodontic Success

Your orthodontist’s competence is crucial in achieving successful outcomes. Skilled orthodontists apply modern methods and technology to develop precise, predictable, and comfortable treatments. Individualized planning means every patient gets personalized advice from start to finish.

Technology, training, and a patient-centered attitude are all very important for achieving long-lasting and beautiful results.

What to Expect During Your Orthodontic Visit

Your first visit typically includes a digital evaluation and comprehensive oral assessment. This is when your orthodontist will discuss your treatment goals. They may use scans and imaging to create plans that align with your needs. This, in turn, allows our team to help you with functional and aesthetic improvements. You’ll also get clear guidance so you feel confident moving forward.

Orthodontic Aftercare and Maintenance Tips

Maintaining excellent oral hygiene is essential during orthodontic treatment.

You’re advised to brush around your brackets and use flossing tools designed for orthodontic appliances. In case of minor discomfort or loose brackets, temporary at-home remedies can help until you reach your orthodontist.

After treatment, retainers play a crucial role. They uphold alignment and maintain long-term results.

Why Choose a Trusted Orthodontic Team?

A reliable orthodontic team values patient comfort, effective communication, and customized treatment. Accuracy is guaranteed via modern technologies, and a supportive environment empowers patients of any age to enjoy their journey to a healthier smile.

A reliable team makes you feel confident. They help with long-term dental wellness for kids, teens, and adults.

Achieve a Confident, Straighter Smile with Advanced Invisalign Treatment

Invisalign has emerged as one of the most reliable modern methods of orthodontic treatment. It’s a discreet and painless alternative to conventional metal braces. This system lets teenagers and adults improve their smiles and stay confident during the treatment process.

Through current digital advancements, Invisalign provides very precise outcomes that support long-term oral health and overall bite functionality.

What Is Invisalign, and How Does It Work?

Invisalign uses a series of clear, removable aligners. They guide teeth through targeted, gradual movement. These aligners are created using digital imaging and precise 3D scans.

Treatment starts with a comprehensive consultation. This is when our orthodontist uses digital impressions to map out how your teeth will shift over time. You’ll get a set of aligners that you’ll replace at specific intervals. This allows for steady progress until you achieve your final smile.

Benefits of Choosing Invisalign over Traditional Braces

Invisalign offers a number of significant benefits for people who want discreet orthodontic treatment. The aligners are very transparent, so you can enhance your smile without drawing attention to your treatment. Their smooth design reduces irritation. Plus, they’re removable. That means maintaining daily oral hygiene is easier than with fixed appliances.

You can take your aligners out before meals. Additionally, using digital planning to map out efficient tooth movement reduces treatment time for many people.

Who’s a Good Candidate for Invisalign?

Invisalign is effective for mild to moderate crowding, spacing issues, and certain bite irregularities. It’s a perfect solution for working professionals who want a discreet option that fits into their lives. This system is also beneficial for teens who’d like a less conspicuous method they can wear consistently.

Successful treatment requires commitment. So you’ll need to follow your orthodontist’s guidance and wear your aligners for the recommended number of hours each day.

The Invisalign Treatment Process

Step 1: Your consultation includes a full orthodontic examination and a 3D scan to create an accurate digital model of your teeth.

Step 2: Your orthodontist plans an individualized treatment.

Step 3: You’ll return for progress appointments. This is when we ensure your teeth are shifting properly. You’ll also receive updated aligners during these visits.

Step 4: Once treatment is complete, you’ll get retainers to protect your alignment.

Why Choose an Invisalign Specialist?

The difference between orthodontic specialists and general dental providers is that the former can handle complex cases and align bite concerns with precision. By selecting an Invisalign specialist, you can be sure that your treatment is based on high-level orthodontic education and experience in teeth movement.

You’ll feel more confident knowing that your treatment is planned and directed by experts who understand all the aspects of orthodontics.

Affordable Invisalign Treatment Options

Many orthodontic practices offer flexible financing like monthly payment plans, insurance coordination, and financing programs. They’re all designed to help families manage treatment expenses comfortably. You’ll continue to get comprehensive digital planning, frequent monitoring, and quality retainers after treatment.

Maintaining Results after Invisalign

You’ve got a significant role to play in maintaining your new smile after treatment. Retainers help preserve tooth position and protect the investment you made during active alignment.

Oral hygiene helps maintain gum health and prevents problems that may affect tooth stability. Regular checkups with your orthodontist help ensure that your alignment is intact and your retainers are still fitting well.

Invisalign outcomes stay stable and attractive even after treatment is over with regular maintenance.

Why Choose a Trusted Orthodontic Team for Invisalign?

A trusted orthodontic team offers customized treatment. They use advanced digital planning and provide supportive guidance at every stage. Their expertise allows for the creation of comfortable aligners that move teeth with precision. So you’ll get reliable long-term stability.

The technology and compassionate approach make it an ideal experience for anyone who wants a discreet but effective solution for their bite problem.

Transform Your Smile with Modern, Affordable Braces for Kids, Teens, and Adults

Braces are one of the most reliable ways to get a beautiful, healthy smile. Over the years, we’ve moved from chunky metal appliances to comfortable, efficient aligners. Whether you’re dealing with alignment issues, bite problems, or just want to improve your smile’s appearance, today’s braces can make orthodontics effective and empowering.

Understanding How Braces Work

Braces use consistent, gentle pressure to shift your teeth into the right position. The system includes brackets attached to each tooth, archwires that guide tooth movement, and elastics that fine-tune your bite.

Together, these elements gradually move teeth into healthier positions. This corrects issues like crowding, gaps, overbites, and underbites.

Types of Braces Available

Traditional Metal Braces

Metal braces can handle even the toughest orthodontic cases. They’re the most effective and durable option out there. They deliver solid results with consistent control.

Clear Ceramic Braces

Ceramic braces offer a more discreet alternative. They blend in with your natural tooth color. They’re perfect for adults and teens who want subtle treatment.

Damon Braces

Damon braces use self-ligating technology, which means they don’t need elastic ties. This provides quicker movement that’s comfortable and requires fewer adjustments.

Customized Treatment Options

Custom treatment plans are designed based on your age, tooth development, or aesthetic goals.

Braces for Kids: Early Orthodontic Intervention

It’s best to bring your kids in around ages 7 to 9 for early diagnosis. This lets your orthodontist spot any emerging concerns in their early stages.

Early intervention guides jaw growth and helps create space for permanent teeth, reducing the need for extensive treatment later.

Braces for Teens: Discreet and Confident Smiles

Teens want discreet, comfortable treatment that keeps their confidence intact. That’s where options like clear braces and Damon systems come in. They support long-term oral health, giving teens a smile they feel proud to share.

Affordable Braces Options

Modern orthodontic practices deliver flexible solutions like payment plans, insurance arrangements, and financing options designed to fit most budgets. And just because it’s affordable doesn’t mean you have to compromise on quality. You’ll still get high-tech, personalized care with solid clinical expertise and technology.

Why Choose Modern Orthodontic Systems?

Today’s braces offer major advantages, including shorter treatment times and improved comfort. Digital imaging, 3D modeling, and advanced planning tools create accurate treatment plans based on each patient’s needs.

Benefits of Professional Orthodontic Care

Professional orthodontic treatment ensures customized planning designed for precise tooth movement and predictable results. Patients, regardless of age, experience improved overall health and functional benefits as well.

Caring for Your Braces

Caring for braces during treatment is pretty straightforward. Here’s how:

  • Brush and floss daily using tools designed for braces.
  • Avoid food that’s sticky or hard to chew.
  • Make it a habit to visit your orthodontist for routine adjustments.

Why Choose a Trusted Orthodontic Team

Working with a trusted orthodontic team means gaining access to a group with extensive experience. These experts use advanced technology to deliver predictable outcomes. Your trusted team is committed to comfort, communication, and high-quality care.

Trick, Treat & Smile: How to Have a Braces-Friendly Halloween

Trick or Treat with Braces? Yes You Can! — Your Friendly Guide to a Braces-Safe Halloween

Halloween is just around the corner, and we know what that means — costumes, spooky decorations, and of course… CANDY! But if you’re rocking braces this year, you might be wondering:

Can I still enjoy Halloween treats without breaking a bracket?
Absolutely! With a little guidance, you can still have a fang-tastic time while keeping your orthodontic journey on track.

Braces-Safe Candy Options

The golden rule? Avoid anything too hard, sticky, or chewy. But don’t worry — there are still plenty of treats that are braces-approved:

✅ Safe-to-Eat Treats:

  • Plain chocolate bars (no nuts or caramel) – Think Hershey’s, Dove, or milk chocolate squares
  • Peanut butter cups – Soft and melty, these are a braces favorite!
  • Soft cookies – Just make sure they’re not too crunchy
  • Marshmallows – Soft, fluffy, and spooky-approved!
  • M&M’s (without peanuts) – Let them melt in your mouth, not crunch between brackets
  • Three Musketeers or Milky Way (no caramel chunks) – Soft nougat = yes, sticky caramel = no

Pro Tip: When in doubt, go with treats that melt easily or are soft to chew.

❌ Candies to Avoid (aka Braces’ Worst Nightmares):

  • Caramel & taffy – These sticky treats can pull off brackets and wires
  • Gummy candies – Gummy bears, worms, and fruit snacks are a no-go
  • Hard candies – Jolly Ranchers, jawbreakers, and lollipops are bracket-busters
  • Nuts & popcorn – These can bend wires or get painfully stuck
  • Chewy candies – Starburst, Now & Laters, and Skittles are better left to the goblins

5 Fang-Tastic Tips for a Braces-Friendly Halloween

  1. Sort Your Candy
    After trick-or-treating, have a little “candy inspection party” to separate the safe treats from the scary ones.
  2. Brush & Floss After Treats
    Sugar loves to stick around brackets, so brush and floss thoroughly after enjoying your treats.
  3. Use Moderation
    Candy is fun, but try to limit how much (and how often) you snack on it to keep cavities away.
  4. Keep a Braces Emergency Kit Handy
    If something does break or come loose, have wax and our office number ready — just in case!

Still Want to Celebrate? Here Are Some Braces-Friendly Alternatives:

  • Hot cocoa with marshmallows
  • Halloween-themed smoothies
  • Spooky soft cupcakes or pudding cups
  • A movie night with friends (no popcorn!)
  • DIY soft cookie decorating party

Final Thoughts from Our Team:

Having braces during Halloween doesn’t mean missing out on the fun. With a little caution and creativity, you can enjoy the spooky season and stay on track for that perfect smile.

If you ever have a question about what’s safe to eat or if you have a braces mishap this Halloween, don’t ghost us — give us a call!

Happy Halloween from all of us at Valley Orthodontics!
Stay safe, smile bright, and don’t forget to brush!
